description.en: The single bound a value is tested against (a threshold boundary), in the enclosing concept's unit. Named `bound`, not `value` — `value` is reserved for an actual magnitude.
Which side of the threshold trips: above ⇒ trips when the reading exceeds `at`; below ⇒ when it drops under. Borrowed from HA numeric_state (above/below).
Optional deadband around `at` to suppress chatter — once tripped, the reading must travel this far back past `at` to clear. Same unit as `at`; never fabricate.
